learning_objective: Students will compare the product lifecycle of traditional software (which ships, stabilizes, and reaches end-of-life) with perpetual beta products (which never leave development), examining how the beta label prevents accountability.
specification:
Type: chart **sim-id:** perpetual-beta-lifecycle
**Library:** Chart.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Taxonomy:** Analyze (L4) **Bloom Verb:** Compare, Examine **Learning Objective:** Students will compare the product lifecycle of traditional software (which ships, stabilizes, and reaches end-of-life) with perpetual beta products (which never leave development), examining how the beta label prevents accountability. Chart type: Dual-line chart comparing two lifecycle patterns X-axis: Time (Year 1 through Year 10) Y-axis: Product maturity percentage (0-100%) Data series: 1. "Traditional Software" (blue line): Rises from 10% to 90% over years 1-4, stabilizes at 90-95% for years 5-8, slight decline in years 9-10 (end-of-life) 2. "Perpetual Beta Product" (gold dashed line): Rises from 10% to 40% in years 1-3, oscillates between 35-55% for years 4-10, never exceeds 60% 3. Horizontal red dotted line at 80%: "Minimum viable shipping threshold" Annotations: - Arrow at Traditional year 3: "Version 1.0 ships" - Arrow at Perpetual Beta year 5: "Still in beta — 'exciting updates coming'" - Arrow at Perpetual Beta year 8: "Major rewrite announced — 'even better beta'" - Shaded region above 80%: "The zone crypto never reaches" Interactive features: - Hover over data points for descriptions of what happens at each stage - Toggle: Show/hide a third line for "Vaporware" (stays at 5-10% forever) - Responsive to container width Instructional Rationale: Dual-line comparison makes the perpetual beta pattern visually distinct from normal product development. The "shipping threshold" line provides a reference point for evaluation, supporting Analyze-level pattern recognition. Implementation: Chart.js with line chart, annotation plugin, and custom tooltip callbacks. Responsive container.
